selection: new nuclear power plant in Switzerland
description: Argumente und Zahlen pro und kontra Neubau von Kernkraftwerken in der Schweiz (Stand der Debatte 2025/2026, nach Aufhebung des Neubauverbots durch das Parlament). Pro- und Kontra-Argumente sind als Triples modelliert, belegt durch Werte mit Quellen; einige Rechnungen sind als calc-validation hinterlegt.

words.name: Switzerland
words.description: a landlocked country located in west-central Europe.

words.name: nuclear
words.description: relating to the nucleus of an atom and the energy released in nuclear reactions.

words.name: power
words.description: the rate, per unit time, at which electrical energy is transferred.

words.name: plant
words.description: an industrial facility for the generation of electric power.

words.name: new
words.description: recently built or established, as opposed to existing.

words.name: electricity
words.description: the set of physical phenomena associated with the presence and motion of electric charge.

words.name: production
words.description: the action of making or generating something.

words.name: consumption
words.description: the using up of a resource.

words.name: share
words.description: a part or portion of a larger amount.

words.name: import
words.description: a good or commodity brought into a country from abroad.

words.name: export
words.description: a good or commodity sent out of a country.

words.name: argument
words.description: a reason or set of reasons given in support of an idea or position.

words.name: pro
words.description: an argument or consideration in favour of something.

words.name: contra
words.description: an argument or consideration against something.

words.name: supply
words.description: the amount of a resource available for use.

words.name: security
words.description: the state of being free from danger or threat.

words.name: winter
words.description: the coldest season of the year, in Switzerland the period of highest electricity demand and lowest solar yield.

words.name: gap
words.description: an unfilled space or interval; here a shortfall between supply and demand.

words.name: greenhouse
words.description: relating to gases that trap heat in the atmosphere.

words.name: gas
words.description: a state of matter; here shorthand for a greenhouse gas emission.

words.name: emission
words.description: the production and discharge of something, especially gas.

words.name: lifecycle
words.description: the whole series of stages over the life of a product or facility.

words.name: capacity
words.description: the maximum output an installation can produce.

words.name: factor
words.description: a ratio; here the ratio of actual to maximum possible output.

words.name: baseload
words.description: the minimum level of demand on an electrical grid over a span of time, met by continuous generation.

words.name: levelized
words.description: averaged over the lifetime of an asset.

words.name: cost
words.description: the amount paid or required for something.

words.name: energy
words.description: the capacity to do work, here electrical energy.

words.name: construction
words.description: the building of an installation.

words.name: time
words.description: the measured duration of an event.

words.name: decommissioning
words.description: the process of shutting down and dismantling a nuclear facility.

words.name: disposal
words.description: the action of getting rid of waste, here radioactive waste.

words.name: waste
words.description: unwanted or unusable material; here spent nuclear fuel and contaminated material.

words.name: uranium
words.description: a chemical element used as nuclear fuel.

words.name: fuel
words.description: material burned or consumed to produce energy.

words.name: dependency
words.description: the state of relying on something external.

words.name: solar
words.description: relating to energy from the sun.

words.name: photovoltaic
words.description: converting light directly into electricity.

words.name: wind
words.description: the use of air flow to generate electricity.

words.name: onshore
words.description: located on land, as opposed to offshore.

words.name: renewable
words.description: an energy source that is naturally replenished.

words.name: Beznau
words.description: the oldest Swiss nuclear power station, in the canton of Aargau.

words.name: Gösgen
words.description: a Swiss nuclear power station in the canton of Solothurn.

words.name: Leibstadt
words.description: the newest and most powerful Swiss nuclear power station, in the canton of Aargau.

words.name: shutdown
words.description: the permanent ceasing of operation of a plant.

words.name: year
words.description: the orbital period of the Earth around the Sun; the standard period word.

words.name: TWh
words.description: terawatt-hour, a unit of energy equal to one billion kilowatt-hours.

words.name: percent
words.description: a proportion expressed as a fraction of 100.

words.name: USD
words.description: the United States dollar.

words.name: MWh
words.description: megawatt-hour, a unit of energy equal to one thousand kilowatt-hours.

words.name: CHF
words.description: the Swiss franc.

words.name: bn
words.description: billion, a scaling word for 1e9.

words.name: CO2eq
words.description: carbon-dioxide equivalent, a measure of greenhouse-gas emission expressed as the equivalent mass of CO2.

words.name: kWh
words.description: kilowatt-hour, a unit of energy.

words.name: gram
words.description: a metric unit of mass.
the calendar year 2024.
the calendar year 2032.
the calendar year 2040.
the calendar year 2045.
the calendar year 2025.

words.name: lower bound estimate
words.description: the low end of an estimated range.

words.name: upper bound estimate
words.description: the high end of an estimated range.

words.name: social
words.description: relating to society and external effects beyond the private actor.

words.name: adder
words.description: an additive contribution to a total, here a per-kWh cost component; positive raises the cost, negative lowers it.

words.name: mean
words.description: the expected (average) value of an estimate.

words.name: shadow
words.description: an imputed price for a non-market effect, used to monetise it.

words.name: price
words.description: the amount of money expected or given for something.

words.name: CO2
words.description: carbon dioxide, the principal anthropogenic greenhouse gas.

words.name: tonne
words.description: a metric unit of mass equal to 1000 kilograms.

words.name: Rappen
words.description: one hundredth of a Swiss franc.

words.name: duration
words.description: the length of time something continues.

words.name: delay
words.description: a period by which something is late or postponed.

triples.name: nuclear power plant
triples.description: a thermal power station in which the heat source is a nuclear reactor.

triples.name: new nuclear power plant
triples.description: a newly built nuclear power plant, as opposed to an existing one.

triples.name: new nuclear power plant in Switzerland
triples.description: the proposal to build one or more new nuclear power plants in Switzerland after the 2025 lifting of the construction ban.

triples.name: nuclear electricity production share
triples.description: the share of total electricity production supplied by nuclear power.

triples.name: pro argument for new nuclear in Switzerland
triples.to: new nuclear power plant in Switzerland

triples.name: contra argument for new nuclear in Switzerland
triples.to: new nuclear power plant in Switzerland

triples.name: winter supply gap
triples.description: the shortfall between electricity demand and domestic supply during winter months.

triples.name: lifecycle greenhouse gas emission
triples.description: greenhouse-gas emissions over the full lifecycle of a generation technology, per unit of energy.

triples.name: levelized cost of energy
triples.description: the average net present cost of electricity generation over the lifetime of a generator, per unit of energy.

triples.name: decommissioning and disposal cost
triples.description: the combined cost of decommissioning nuclear plants and disposing of radioactive waste.

triples.name: uranium import dependency
triples.description: Switzerland imports the entirety of its nuclear fuel, having no domestic uranium.

triples.name: Swiss nuclear fleet production
triples.description: the combined annual electricity production of all Swiss nuclear power plants.

triples.name: Swiss nuclear fleet shutdown loss
triples.description: the combined annual electricity production lost once all Swiss nuclear power plants are shut down on schedule; the result of the fleet production loss sum formula.

triples.name: lifecycle greenhouse gas emission of nuclear power
lifecycle greenhouse gas emission of solar photovoltaic
levelized cost of energy of nuclear power
levelized cost of energy of solar photovoltaic
levelized cost of energy of onshore wind
CO2eq per kWh
triples.description: grams of CO2-equivalent emitted per kilowatt-hour generated.

triples.name: USD per MWh
triples.description: US dollars of cost per megawatt-hour generated.

triples.name: CHF bn
triples.description: billions of Swiss francs.

triples.name: CHF per kWh
triples.description: Swiss francs of cost per kilowatt-hour generated; the common unit of every cost adder.

triples.name: CHF per tonne CO2
triples.description: the shadow price of carbon, Swiss francs per tonne of CO2.

triples.name: social cost adder
triples.description: one monetised per-kWh cost component (private or external) entering the total social cost of new Swiss nuclear power.

triples.name: social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland
triples.to: new nuclear power plant in Switzerland
triples.description: the weighted total cost per kWh of new Swiss nuclear power, summing every monetised pro and contra adder.

triples.name: levelized cost adder
triples.description: contra/base: the direct generation cost (Lazard LCOE midpoint), the dominant positive adder.

triples.name: decommissioning cost adder
triples.description: contra: decommissioning + waste-disposal cost amortised over lifetime generation.

triples.name: carbon benefit adder
triples.description: pro: avoided-emission benefit vs the marginal alternative, monetised at the carbon shadow price; enters as a negative adder.

triples.name: supply security adder
triples.description: pro: monetised value of firm winter baseload (avoided scarcity / import-price-spike cost); enters as a negative adder.

triples.name: uranium dependency adder
triples.description: contra: monetised geopolitical/supply risk of 100% imported uranium fuel; positive adder.

triples.name: construction delay adder
triples.description: contra: monetised cost of the long build/delay risk (capital tied up, gap bridged by other means); positive adder.

sources.name: GRS Swiss nuclear overview 2024
sources.description: GRS gGmbH country overview: Switzerland generated over 82 TWh in 2024, 29.2% from nuclear; net exporter (40.35 TWh export vs 25.95 TWh import).

sources.name: Wikipedia Kernenergie in der Schweiz
sources.description: Decommissioning and waste-disposal cost study (swissnuclear/BFE), updated estimate of CHF 23.9 bn (Dec 2020); shutdown schedule Beznau 2032/33.

sources.name: FuW Energiezukunft Schweiz 2026
sources.description: Finanz und Wirtschaft op-ed: Swiss fleet ~23 TWh; Beznau -5.5 TWh (2032/33), Gösgen -8 TWh (~2040), Leibstadt -9.5 TWh (~2045).

sources.name: Lazard LCOE+ 2025
sources.description: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy+ 2025 (18th ed.), unsubsidised USD/MWh: nuclear 141-220, utility-scale solar 38-217, onshore wind 37-86.

sources.name: IPCC AR5 WG3 Annex III
sources.description: IPCC lifecycle GHG emission medians (g CO2eq/kWh): nuclear 12, solar PV utility 48.

sources.name: bluewin Swiss reactor build 2026
sources.description: Parliament lifted the construction ban; a new Swiss plant is unlikely to be on the grid before 2045.

sources.name: BAFU CO2 levy 2025
sources.description: Swiss CO2 levy on fossil fuels 2025: CHF 120 per tonne CO2, capped under the revised CO2 act; study scenarios (INFRAS) model 210-760 CHF/t.

values.note: Swiss CO2 levy 2025, capped at CHF 120/t; used as the low anchor of the carbon shadow price. Study scenarios reach 210-760 CHF/t, hence the wide lower/upper band on the carbon benefit adder.
Lazard nuclear LCOE midpoint (141+220)/2 = 180.5 USD/MWh, converted at ~0.90 USD/CHF -> 0.1625 CHF/kWh. Positive (cost).
mean minus one sigma (0.04, the 141-220 USD/MWh band plus FX uncertainty).
mean plus one sigma (0.04, the 141-220 USD/MWh band plus FX uncertainty).
CHF 23.9 bn over ~1150 TWh lifetime fleet generation (23 TWh/a x 50 a). Positive (cost).
mean minus one sigma (0.008, cost-overrun and lifetime-output uncertainty).
mean plus one sigma (0.008; disposal estimates have historically risen).
Avoided emissions vs a partly-fossil marginal alternative (~25 g CO2eq/kWh net benefit) at ~120 CHF/t -> ~0.003 CHF/kWh saved. Negative (benefit). Small because Swiss grid is already low-carbon.
mean minus one sigma (0.004); larger avoided-emission benefit at the upper carbon price (up to 760 CHF/t).
mean plus one sigma (0.004); sign flips to a small cost if PV/wind is the true counterfactual.
Imputed value of firm winter baseload (avoided import-price spikes / scarcity). Negative (benefit). Highly uncertain shadow value, not a market price.
mean minus one sigma (0.02); high imputed value of firm winter baseload.
mean plus one sigma (0.02); least market-grounded estimate, the band reaches near-zero benefit.
Monetised geopolitical risk premium of 100% imported fuel. Positive (cost). Fuel is a small share of nuclear LCOE, so the premium is modest.
mean minus one sigma (0.005); geopolitical premia are hard to price, band kept as wide as the mean.
mean plus one sigma (0.005); geopolitical premia are hard to price, band kept as wide as the mean.
Monetised cost of long build + delay risk (Flamanville/OL3-type overruns, capital tied up, gap bridged meanwhile). Positive (cost), partly overlapping LCOE.
mean minus one sigma (0.015); overrun distribution is heavily right-skewed, sigma set equal to mean as a rough proxy.
mean plus one sigma (0.015); overrun distribution is heavily right-skewed, sigma set equal to mean as a rough proxy.

formulas.name: fleet production loss sum
formulas.expression: "Swiss nuclear fleet shutdown loss" = "Beznau production loss at shutdown" + "Gösgen production loss at shutdown" + "Leibstadt production loss at shutdown"

formulas.name: social cost per kWh lower formula
formulas.expression: "social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland" = "levelized cost adder" + "decommissioning cost adder" + "carbon benefit adder" + "supply security adder" + "uranium dependency adder" + "construction delay adder"
formulas.latex: c_{\text{soc}}^{-} = c_{\text{LCOE}} + c_{\text{decom}} + c_{\text{CO}_2} + c_{\text{sec}} + c_{\text{U}} + c_{\text{build}}

formulas.name: social cost per kWh formula
formulas.expression: "social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland" = "levelized cost adder" + "decommissioning cost adder" + "carbon benefit adder" + "supply security adder" + "uranium dependency adder" + "construction delay adder"
formulas.latex: c_{\text{soc}} = c_{\text{LCOE}} + c_{\text{decom}} + c_{\text{CO}_2} + c_{\text{sec}} + c_{\text{U}} + c_{\text{build}}

formulas.name: social cost per kWh upper formula
formulas.expression: "social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland" = "levelized cost adder" + "decommissioning cost adder" + "carbon benefit adder" + "supply security adder" + "uranium dependency adder" + "construction delay adder"
formulas.latex: c_{\text{soc}}^{+} = c_{\text{LCOE}} + c_{\text{decom}} + c_{\text{CO}_2} + c_{\text{sec}} + c_{\text{U}} + c_{\text{build}}

calc-validation.note: Combined production lost when all three plants are shut down on schedule: 5.5 + 8 + 9.5 = 23 TWh, matching the ~23 TWh fleet output cited as the supply gap to be replaced.

calc-validation.formula: social cost per kWh lower formula
words: social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland
calc-validation.note: Lower-bound scenario, every adder at mean minus one sigma: 0.1225 + 0.0128 - 0.007 - 0.04 + 0.0 + 0.0 = 0.0883 CHF/kWh.

words: social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland
calc-validation.note: Expected weighted cost, every adder at its mean: 0.1625 + 0.0208 - 0.003 - 0.02 + 0.005 + 0.015 = 0.1803 CHF/kWh. The LCOE adder dominates; the supply-security and construction-delay adders carry the largest relative uncertainty.

calc-validation.formula: social cost per kWh upper formula
words: social cost per kWh of new nuclear in Switzerland
calc-validation.note: Upper-bound scenario, every adder at mean plus one sigma: 0.2025 + 0.0288 + 0.001 + 0.0 + 0.01 + 0.03 = 0.2723 CHF/kWh.
